yesterdayAnalysis: Strong 4.1-mile run in San Diego at 39 TSS with controlled HR of 145 bpm. This completes the key running work for the week before Sunday's race. Good execution of the volume cap (under 5 miles) to protect the hip while maintaining aerobic fitness.
todayRecommendation: Execute the planned 50-55 minute easy-moderate ROUVY spin with no brick. Keep power conversational (Zone 2, ~140-165w), legs fresh, and avoid any intensity. This is pure race-week prep, not a training stimulus.
weekProgress: Week 10 of 10 is properly structured as race week taper for Sunday's SDIT. With 6 days remaining including today, 0 of 300 TSS is exactly on schedule—this week is intentionally light with Friday full rest and Saturday shakeout only. The plan is working as designed.
keyInsight: Race week execution is paramount: today's easy spin keeps the legs turning without fatigue, setting up Sunday's International tri as the true Oregon dress rehearsal for swim-hard, bike-controlled, run-strong pacing strategy.
positives: Friday Oregon full-course brick (114 TSS, 55.7mi) nailed race NP execution and built race-specific confidence
Yesterday's 4.1mi run at HR 145 shows controlled effort and good aerobic economy heading into race week
Sleep quality rebounded to 68 score last night after three consecutive sub-50 nights—timing is perfect for race week freshness
